name: DBV Technologies S.A.
business: DBV Technologies is a late-stage specialty biopharmaceutical company focused on changing the field of immunotherapy by developing a novel technology platform called Viaskin, an epicutaneous patch for delivering biologically active compounds to the immune system through intact skin. Its most advanced product candidate is Viaskin Peanut Patch, evaluated as a potential therapy for children with peanut allergy, with plans to file a BLA in the first half of 2026 for ages 4-7 and in the second half of 2026 for ages 1-3. The company also has an earlier-stage program, Viaskin Milk, for cow's milk allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis.

exposures.sentence: Our most advanced product candidate is Viaskin Peanut Patch, which has been evaluated as a potential therapy for children with peanut allergy in twelve completed clinical trials, including five Phase 2 trials and five completed Phase 3 trials.

catalysts.event: BLA submission for Viaskin Peanut Patch for children 4-7 years old
catalysts.sentence: The Company plans a BLA submission in the first half of 2026 for Viaskin Peanut Patch as a potential treatment for children four to seven years old.

catalysts.event: BLA submission for Viaskin Peanut Patch for children 1-3 years old (conditional on completion of safety study)
catalysts.sentence: Conditional on successful completion of this safety study, the Company plans a BLA submission in the second half of 2026 for children ages one to three.

catalysts.event: Initiation of confirmatory effectiveness study for toddlers 1-3 under Accelerated Approval
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects that the confirmatory study will be initiated by the time of BLA submission and would run in parallel to commercialization in the United States, if the Viaskin Peanut patch is approved.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 670,000 children ages 1 to 7 years old in the U.S. have peanut allergy.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are approximately 670,000 children ages 1 to 7 years old in the U.S. with peanut allergy.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 1.6 million children in the U.S. (2.2% of pediatric population) are allergic to peanuts.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on a 2018 publication, an estimated 2.2% of the pediatric population in the United States, approximately 1.6 million children, is allergic to peanuts.

tamClaims.claim: Prevalence of peanut allergy more than tripled from 0.4% to 1.4% between 1997 and 2008.
tamClaims.sentence: This reflects an increasing prevalence, as has been shown by several epidemiologic studies, including a cross-sectional survey-based study in the United States in which the prevalence of peanut allergy more than tripled between 1997 and 2008 from 0.4% to 1.4%.
